breakingChangesNotes: This version introduces support for the compromised/spd and compromised/masked_card collections. The compromised/masked_card collection is now supported both in the incident integration and in the feed integration. It also updates Incident Field IDs for three previously truncated fields: incidentfield-GIB_Nation-State_Cybercriminals_Threat_Actor_Aliases.json (id changed to gibnationstatecybercriminalsthreatactoraliases), incidentfield-GIB_Scanner_Sources.json (id changed to gibscannersources), and incidentfield-GIB_Phishing_Date_Updated.json (id changed to gibphishingdateupdated); playbooks that referenced the previous broken IDs in incident context must be updated. In addition, duplicate prevention is now available in the Group-IB Threat Intelligence integration fetch flow, but Skip updated incidents is disabled by default and should be enabled only when Pre-Processing Rules are unreliable. Customers who relied on GIBIncidentUpdate or GIBIncidentUpdateIncludingClosed for update propagation should keep Skip updated incidents disabled and re-enable the appropriate Pre-Processing Rule manually. The compromised/mule collection has been removed from the Collections to fetch selector and is no longer exposed as a fetch-incidents configuration option. The `gibtia-local-search` command has been renamed to `gibti-local-search` and no longer accepts the `date_from` and `date_to` arguments; use `seq_update` for update-based iteration control instead. The `gibtia-local-search` command output context paths have also been renamed: `id` is now `GIBTI.search.local.id` and `additional_info` is now `GIBTI.search.local.additional_info` (alongside the existing `GIBTI.search.local.seqUpdate` and `GIBTI.search.local.raw_feed`). Playbooks that referenced the previous top-level `id` / `additional_info` paths must be updated to use the new namespaced paths. Several content items had their fromversion bumped.
